title: Chunk curriculum into smaller pieces/chapters with a dependency tree & table of contents in a separate md file so we can parse it quicker
description:
The induction_curriculum.md file is currently 3897 lines and difficult to parse/navigate. Refactor to improve organization:

OBJECTIVES:
- Split curriculum into modular chapter files (one file per lesson or per phase)
- Create master table of contents with dependency tree
- Improve parsability and navigation
- Maintain all existing content

PROPOSED STRUCTURE:
- curriculum/README.md (TOC + dependency visualization)
- curriculum/phase1/ (lessons 01-10)
- curriculum/phase2/ (lessons 11-18)
- curriculum/phase3/ (lessons 19-26)
- curriculum/phase4/ (lessons 27-32)
- curriculum/phase5/ (lessons 33-38)
- curriculum/phase6/ (lessons 39-44)

BENEFITS:
- Faster file parsing (smaller files)
- Better git diffs (changes isolated to specific lessons)
- Easier navigation with TOC
- Clear dependency visualization
- Modular updates without conflicts

REQUIREMENTS:
- Preserve all 68 photo placeholders
- Maintain cross-references between lessons
- Generate dependency graph (e.g., Lesson 28 depends on Lesson 27)
- Include metadata (duration, prerequisites, tools needed)
- Keep original file as reference until migration complete

title: Level 6: Full System Integration & Validation
description: Complete end-to-end system integration connecting all subsystems. Verify full control loop operation, safety interlocks, thermal management, EMI filtering, and system-level performance. Validate against design specifications (1500-2000W, temperature accuracy, ZVS operation, protection response times).

title: Verify complete power delivery path: AC mains to pan heating
description: Simulate full power path integration: AC input → rectifier → DC bus → half-bridge → resonant tank → pan load. Verify end-to-end power conversion efficiency, harmonic distortion (THD), power factor, inrush current, and transient response to load changes. Measure losses in each stage and identify optimization opportunities.

title: Verify complete control loop: sensing to actuation
description: Integrate complete control loop: temperature/current sensing → ESP32-S3 ADC → PID controller → PWM generation → gate drivers → power stage. Verify closed-loop stability, step response, disturbance rejection, sensor-to-actuator latency (<10ms target), and temperature control accuracy (±0.5°C). Reference Lessons 27-31.

title: Verify safety interlock system and fault response
description: Integrate all safety features: overcurrent detection (hardware + software), overtemperature shutdown, UVLO, overvoltage protection, pan detection, watchdog timer. Verify fault detection latency, protection threshold accuracy, safe-state fallback, and fault recovery procedures. Reference Lessons 22, 27, 30.

title: Verify EMI/EMC performance and filtering effectiveness
description: Simulate EMI filter design (common-mode, differential-mode) at AC input. Verify conducted emissions compliance (EN 55014-1), switching noise suppression, ground loop prevention, and shield effectiveness. Measure noise coupling to sensing circuits and verify filtering adequacy. Predict radiated emissions hotspots.

title: Verify system-level thermal management under worst-case conditions
description: Simulate complete thermal system: IGBT losses, LMR51430 thermal, capacitor heating, enclosure temperature rise, fan control. Verify thermal runaway prevention, derating curves, continuous operation at rated power (2kW), and thermal shutdown thresholds. Test worst-case: 85°C ambient, no pan (no-load), maximum power.

title: Verify system meets all design specifications and performance targets
description: Final validation against design requirements: 1500-2000W power range, temperature accuracy ±0.5°C, ZVS operation >90% duty range, efficiency >85%, startup time <5s, temperature settling time <60s, fault response <100ms, isolation >2.5kV, EN 55014 compliance. Generate comprehensive test report documenting compliance.

title: Induction Cooker Development
description: Development of a production-grade, high-precision induction heating system based on the Simulation-First methodology. Includes Phases I-VI.

title: Lesson 01: Setup Simulation Environment
description: Establish version-controlled KiCad 8.0 project with hierarchical sheets (Power_Input, Resonant_Tank, Aux_Power, MCU_Control, Sensing_UI). Configure ngspice integration.

title: Lesson 02: Validate SPICE Fundamentals
description: Correlate simple R-LED network simulation with theory. Validate netlist-to-schematic workflow.

title: Lesson 03: Gate Charge & Miller Plateau
description: Simulate capacitive loading of MOSFET/IGBT gate. Compare weak (MCU) vs strong gate drive. Validate rise times (target ~30ns for strong drive).

title: Lesson 04: IKW40N120H3 Characterization
description: Create/Validate SPICE model for IKW40N120H3. Perform Double Pulse Test. Measure Eoff (target 0.9mJ), Tail current (~200ns), and Vce overshoot.

title: Lesson 05: UCC21550 Gate Driver Characterization
description: Simulate UCC21550 propagation delay, isolation, and dead-time. Verify dead time > 300ns to prevent shoot-through. Test CMTI immunity.

title: Lesson 06: LMR51430 Buck Converter Design
description: Simulate 24V->5V aux supply. Validate transient response (load step 100mA->600mA). Components: L=10uH, C=22uF. Verify Vout > 4.75V during dip.

title: Lesson 07: Low-Noise 3.3V LDO Design
description: Design 3.3V rail for ADC. Pi-Filter (L=1uH, C=10uF) + LDO. Target >60dB attenuation at 1.1MHz.

title: Lesson 08: UCC14140-Q1 Isolated Supply
description: Simulate isolated supply for high-side gate driver. VDD-VEE=22V, COM-VEE=4V. Verify isolation capacitance <3.5pF and CMTI >150kV/us.

title: Lesson 09: AC Input Stage Design
description: Design full-bridge rectifier and soft-start circuit (NTC/Resistor + Relay). Limit inrush < 20A. Include input filter and protection.

title: Create grounding and EMI strategy document
description: Define grounding architecture for mixed high-power/low-signal system. Document: earth ground connection, star grounding for analog sensing, power ground vs signal ground separation, isolation barrier ground treatment, shield termination strategy. Address EMI filtering requirements per IEC 60335-2-6. Create grounding diagram showing all ground domains and connections.

title: Verify selected components in the induction_curriculum.md can actually work together for the intended project (which is documented in the md file). The datasheet pdfs are in the md file. Use reasonable strategies to prove these components make sense to go together.
Level 4: Resonant Tank & Load Integration
description: Integrate resonant tank (coil, capacitor bank, pan load model) with half-bridge power stage. Verify resonant frequency tracking, ZVS operation, current waveforms, reactive power handling, and power transfer efficiency across load variations.

title: Verify resonant tank component design (L, C values)
description: Design and verify resonant tank components: work coil inductance (L), series resonant capacitor bank (C), resonant frequency calculation (30-40kHz range). Verify Q factor, impedance characteristics, and frequency sensitivity. Reference Lesson 11 resonance theory and Lesson 12 capacitor bank design.

title: Verify pan load modeling and coupling coefficient
description: Simulate pan load as transformer-equivalent circuit per Lesson 13. Verify coupling coefficient (k), mutual inductance, reflected impedance to primary coil. Model various pan materials (stainless steel, cast iron) and pan sizes. Verify power transfer efficiency and load variation impact on resonant frequency.

title: Verify ZVS operation and soft-switching waveforms
description: Integrate half-bridge power stage with resonant tank. Verify Zero Voltage Switching (ZVS) conditions: drain voltage zero-crossing before gate turn-on, capacitive current for ZVS, dead-time optimization for ZVS maintenance. Measure switching losses with/without ZVS. Reference Lesson 15 ZVS analysis.

title: Verify reactive power handling and capacitor stress
description: Simulate resonant capacitor bank under full power operation (2kW). Verify RMS current ratings, voltage stress (peak, RMS), ESR power dissipation, thermal rise. Confirm capacitor selection meets reactive power requirements (VARs) and reliability margins. Reference Lesson 12 capacitor bank design.

title: Verify power transfer efficiency across operating range
description: Simulate complete resonant tank + power stage across full operating range: 200W to 2000W, various pan loads, frequency variations (±5kHz). Measure overall efficiency (AC input to pan heating), identify loss mechanisms (switching, conduction, copper, core), optimize for maximum efficiency. Generate efficiency vs power curves.

title: Verify LMR51430 thermal management strategy
description: Address the thermal concern from COMPONENT_COMPATIBILITY_VERIFICATION.md where LMR51430 reaches 150°C limit in worst-case (70°C ambient). Simulate with thermal model including: copper pour heat spreading (θJA ~60°C/W), component placement away from hot zones, thermal derating curves. Determine if additional mitigation (forced air, heatsink) is needed.

title: Level 5: Sensing & Monitoring Integration
description: Integrate all sensing subsystems: current transformer, MAX31865 temperature sensing, high-voltage bus monitoring. Verify measurement accuracy, isolation integrity, noise filtering, and fault detection thresholds. Connect to ESP32-S3 ADC and digital interfaces.

title: Verify current transformer sensing circuit
description: Simulate current transformer (CT) for tank current measurement. Verify turns ratio, burden resistor design, frequency response (DC to 100kHz), linearity, and phase accuracy. Design signal conditioning for ESP32-S3 ADC input (0-3.3V range). Include overcurrent detection threshold circuitry. Reference Lesson 17.

title: Verify MAX31865 RTD temperature sensing with isolation
description: Simulate MAX31865 RTD converter interfaced to PT100/PT1000 probe with SPI isolation. Verify measurement accuracy (±0.1°C target), noise rejection (50/60Hz), 2/3/4-wire RTD configurations, fault detection (open/short circuit), and calibration procedure. Include ADUM1250 isolation for high-voltage safety.

title: Verify high-voltage bus monitoring with isolation
description: Design and verify isolated voltage sensing for 310V DC bus monitoring. Use resistive divider + isolation amplifier or optocoupler. Verify voltage accuracy (±5%), isolation rating (>2.5kV), bandwidth for transient detection, and overvoltage/undervoltage threshold detection. Reference Lesson 10.

title: Verify NTC thermistor sensing for thermal management
description: Simulate NTC thermistor interface for IGBT heatsink and enclosure temperature monitoring. Design voltage divider, linearization, and calibration. Verify temperature range (-40°C to +125°C), accuracy (±2°C), response time, and ADC interface. Reference Lesson 20.

title: Verify complete sensing subsystem integration with ESP32-S3
description: Integrate all sensing circuits (CT, MAX31865, HV monitor, NTC) with ESP32-S3 ADC and digital interfaces. Verify simultaneous sampling, channel crosstalk, common-mode noise rejection, grounding strategy, and shield termination. Test fault condition propagation and safety interlock triggering.

title: Refine project plan
description: This project is aimed at creating a clone of a breville control freak. It's being built by a relative novice so the build is being broken down into a course. The course starts from zero and builds up to a functional clone. The project prioritizes modularity, iteration, verification, simulation, and safety.
design:
## Architecture

The project is a **simulation-first, modular induction cooking system** designed as a 44-lesson educational curriculum. It guides developers from basic component characterization through full system integration, emphasizing safety, verification, and professional engineering practices.

**System Overview:**
- **Target:** 1500-2000W high-precision induction cooker (Breville Control Freak clone)
- **Methodology:** Simulation-first (validate in SPICE before hardware)
- **Controller:** ESP32-S3 dual-core MCU
- **Power Stage:** Half-bridge resonant inverter (30-40kHz)
- **Safety:** Hardware interlocks + firmware redundancy + galvanic isolation

**Six-Phase Structure:**
1. **Phase I (Lessons 1-10):** Simulation environment & component characterization
2. **Phase II (Lessons 11-18):** Power stage simulation & resonant tank design
3. **Phase III (Lessons 19-26):** Control system & sensing simulation
4. **Phase IV (Lessons 27-32):** Firmware development & algorithm validation
5. **Phase V (Lessons 33-38):** PCB design & manufacturing preparation
6. **Phase VI (Lessons 39-44):** Hardware assembly, integration & calibration

## Components

### Power Electronics
- **IKW40N120H3:** 1200V 40A IGBT (2× for half-bridge)
- **UCC21550:** Isolated gate driver with hardware dead-time
- **Resonant Tank:** 50µH coil + 470nF capacitor bank (33kHz resonance)

### Auxiliary Power
- **LMR51430:** 24V→5V wide-Vin buck converter (1.1MHz switching)
- **LDO + Pi-filter:** 5V→3.3V low-noise supply for MCU
- **UCC14140-Q1:** 12V→22V/4V isolated dual-output for high-side driver

### Sensing & Control
- **ESP32-S3:** Dual-core MCU with MCPWM peripheral
- **MAX31865:** RTD-to-digital converter (15-bit resolution)
- **ADuM1250:** I2C isolator for probe safety
- **AMC1200:** Isolated current sensing amplifier
- **CT:** Current transformer for tank current monitoring

### Safety & UI
- **Hardware Interlocks:** LM393 comparator for over-temperature/over-current
- **Fan Control:** PWM-controlled active cooling with tachometer feedback
- **User Interface:** Rotary encoder + OLED/LCD display
- **RTD Probe:** Galvanically isolated temperature sensor

### Mechanical/Thermal
- **Heatsinks:** Thermal design for 2kW dissipation
- **Active Cooling:** Temperature-controlled fan
- **Thermal Interface:** Thermal vias, PCB copper pours

## Dependencies

**Software/Tools:**
- KiCad 8.0 (schematic capture, PCB layout, integrated SPICE simulation)
- ngspice (circuit simulation engine)
- ESP-IDF (ESP32 firmware development framework)
- Python 3.x (behavioral modeling, PID tuning scripts)
- Manufacturer SPICE models (IKW40N120H3, UCC21550, LMR51430, UCC14140-Q1)

**Design Constraints:**
- AC Input: 120Vrms 60Hz (US mains)
- DC Bus Voltage: 310V (rectified, with PFC assumed)
- Operating Frequency: 30-40kHz (adjustable for ZVS tuning)
- Target Temperature Control: ±0.5°C steady-state error, zero overshoot
- Isolation Requirements: >10MΩ @ 500V (user-accessible probe)
- Safety Standards: UL/IEC compliance (assumed, not explicitly documented)

**Knowledge Prerequisites:**
- Basic circuit theory (Ohm's law, AC analysis, impedance)
- SPICE simulation fundamentals
- Resonant converter theory (LC tanks, ZVS)
- Microcontroller programming (C/C++)
- PCB design principles (isolation, thermal management)
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] Lesson 29 (PLL) implementation completed with detailed algorithm
- [ ] Lesson 31 (State Machine) completed with full state transition diagram

- [ ] Soft-start circuit (Lesson 09) expanded with relay bypass and thermal modeling

- [ ] Component derating calculations documented
- [ ] Firmware code examples completed for all critical algorithms

- [ ] Debugging/troubleshooting guide added
- [ ] BOM cost analysis section added

title: Complete Lesson 29: PLL implementation for ZVS tracking
description:
Lesson 29 currently has only section headers. Need to add:
- PLL theory and phase detection methods
- Digital PLL implementation on ESP32-S3
- Frequency tracking algorithm for maintaining ZVS
- SPICE/Python simulation of PLL behavior
- Code examples for ESP32 timer integration
- Validation criteria and test procedures

title: Complete Lesson 31: State machine diagram and implementation
description:
Lesson 31 lists states and transitions but lacks visual diagram. Need to:
- Create comprehensive state transition diagram
- Document error recovery sequences
- Add detailed state entry/exit conditions
- Include timing diagrams for critical transitions
- Provide complete C implementation example
- Add test cases for state machine validation

title: Add EMI/EMC filtering and compliance section
description:
Missing dedicated EMI/EMC lesson. Should include:
- Conducted emissions filtering (CM/DM chokes)
- Radiated emissions mitigation strategies
- PCB layout rules for high-frequency noise
- Common-mode vs. differential-mode analysis
- SPICE simulation of filter effectiveness
- Regulatory compliance checklist (UL/FCC/CE)
- Measurement procedures and acceptance criteria

title: Expand Lesson 09: Soft-start circuit with relay bypass
description:
Lesson 09 is minimal, only covers basic NTC model. Needs:
- Detailed NTC thermistor thermal time-constant analysis
- Relay bypass circuit design and timing
- PTC vs NTC comparison and selection criteria
- Inrush current calculations
- SPICE simulation with thermal coupling
- Relay contact protection (snubber/MOV)
- Failure mode analysis (stuck relay, open NTC)

title: Add manufacturing tolerance and worst-case analysis
description:
Missing critical manufacturing quality analysis. Need to add:
- Component tolerance stack-up analysis
- Worst-case temperature sweep validation (-40°C to +85°C)
- Component derating calculations (IGBT, capacitors, magnetics)
- Sensitivity analysis for critical parameters (resonant frequency, dead-time)
- Production yield modeling
- Statistical process control metrics
- Qualification test procedures (thermal cycling, vibration, humidity)

title: Generate and integrate 68 photo placeholders
description:
Curriculum has 68 [PHOTO PLACEHOLDER] markers needing real screenshots. Categories:
- KiCad schematic screenshots (symbol chooser, properties, hierarchical sheets)
- Simulation waveforms (oscilloscope plots, bode plots)
- SPICE simulation results (transient analysis, AC sweep)
- 3D PCB renders (layout, stackup visualization)
- Physical hardware photos (breadboard tests, final assembly)
- Measurement instrumentation (multimeter, oscilloscope captures)

This task requires:
1. Building actual KiCad schematics for each lesson
2. Running simulations and capturing results
3. Taking high-quality screenshots with annotations
4. Replacing placeholder text with figure references

title: Document component selection rationale and BOM
description:
Add section explaining why specific components were chosen:
- Cost vs. performance tradeoffs
- Alternative components considered
- Availability and supply chain considerations
- Second-source options for critical components
- Complete BOM with:
  * Manufacturer part numbers
  * Quantity required
  * Unit cost and total project cost
  * Supplier/distributor information
  * Lead time considerations
  * Recommended substitutes

title: Add debugging and troubleshooting guide
description:
Create comprehensive troubleshooting section covering:
- Common failure modes and symptoms
- Debug procedures for each subsystem
- Test point locations and expected values
- Oscilloscope probe placement guidelines
- Error code interpretation
- Safety precautions during debug
- Tools and equipment required
- Step-by-step debug workflows for:
  * No power
  * No heating
  * Erratic temperature control
  * Hardware faults triggering
  * Communication failures

title: Complete firmware code examples for all algorithms
description:
Expand firmware sections with complete, production-ready C implementations:
- Pan detection algorithm (Lesson 27)
- PID controller with anti-windup (Lesson 28)
- PLL for frequency tracking (Lesson 29)
- Watchdog timer integration (Lesson 30)
- State machine (Lesson 31)
- Integration and testing framework (Lesson 32)

Each should include:
- Well-commented source code
- Unit test examples
- Performance benchmarks (CPU usage, timing)
- Memory usage analysis
- Configuration parameters and tuning guidelines

title: Level 1: Auxiliary Power Supply Integration
description: Verify the complete auxiliary power supply chain from AC input through to 3.3V regulation. This includes AC rectifier, LMR51430 buck converter, and 3.3V LDO. Verify voltage regulation, load transient response, ripple characteristics, and thermal performance under realistic load conditions matching the load budget from COMPONENT_COMPATIBILITY_VERIFICATION.md.

title: Verify AC rectifier + soft-start with LMR51430 input
description: Simulate AC mains rectification (120/240VAC) with soft-start circuit feeding LMR51430 buck converter input (12-24V range). Verify voltage ripple at rectifier output, soft-start timing, inrush current limiting, and compatibility with LMR51430 input specs. Include decoupling capacitors per LMR51430 datasheet Section 8.2.1.

title: Verify LMR51430 buck converter with realistic load profile
description: Simulate LMR51430 converting 12-24V to 5V at loads from 100mA to 1.2A (per load budget). Verify output voltage regulation (±2%), load transient response, switching ripple (<50mV), thermal performance (TJ), and loop stability. Include all recommended input/output capacitors and bootstrap components per datasheet Section 8.2.2.

title: Verify 5V to 3.3V LDO with ESP32/peripheral loads
description: Simulate LDO regulator (TBD: select specific part) converting 5V to 3.3V at 380mA input (254mA output per load budget). Verify dropout voltage, line/load regulation, noise performance (<50µV RMS), thermal dissipation, and PSRR. Include input/output decoupling per ESP32-S3 hardware design guidelines.

title: Verify complete aux power chain transient response
description: Simulate complete power-up sequence from AC mains through to stable 3.3V output. Verify power-good sequencing, load step transients (ESP32 WiFi bursts: 150mA baseline to 350mA peak), cross-regulation between 5V and 3.3V rails, and voltage ripple coupling. Confirm all subsystem load requirements are met simultaneously.

title: Level 3: Half-Bridge Power Stage Integration
description: Integrate UCC21550 gate drivers with IKW40N120H3 IGBTs in half-bridge configuration. Verify gate charge delivery, switching waveforms, dead-time adequacy, voltage overshoot suppression, and thermal performance. Include snubber design verification.

title: Verify single IGBT + gate driver switching characteristics
description: Simulate UCC21550 driving single IKW40N120H3 IGBT with resistive load. Measure gate charge delivery waveform, VGE rise/fall times, turn-on/turn-off delays, gate resistor optimization (2.2Ω), and Miller plateau characteristics. Verify gate drive voltage headroom (15V vs 6.5V VGE(th) max). Compare with Lesson 03/04 predictions.

title: Verify half-bridge configuration with dead-time
description: Simulate complete half-bridge: two IKW40N120H3 IGBTs with UCC21550 dual-channel gate drivers. Verify complementary switching with dead-time (700-1000ns), shoot-through prevention, bus voltage handling (310V DC), and switching node voltage excursion (0 to VBUS). Measure dV/dt stress on IGBTs and gate drivers.

title: Verify snubber network design and voltage overshoot suppression
description: Design and simulate RC/RCD snubber networks across IGBTs and half-bridge output. Optimize snubber values to limit voltage overshoot to <20% (372V max from 310V nominal). Verify energy dissipation in snubber resistors, impact on switching losses, and EMI reduction. Reference Lesson 16 design methodology.

title: Verify power stage thermal performance and heatsink requirements
description: Calculate and verify IGBT thermal performance: conduction losses, switching losses, junction temperature under continuous 2kW operation. Verify against Lesson 18 thermal model. Design heatsink requirements (thermal resistance, airflow). Confirm TJ < 150°C with margin under worst-case ambient (85°C).

title: Improve EMI filtering for ESP32-S3 to UCC21550 PWM interface
description:
The current 51Ω + 33pF RC filter provides only 3.26dB attenuation at 100MHz, which may be insufficient in harsh EMI environments near the switch node. Consider design improvements:

**Current Performance:**
- RC filter: R=51Ω, C=33pF (fc ≈ 94MHz)
- 100MHz noise rejection: 3.26dB (31% reduction)
- Verified in sim_10_esp32_ucc21550_pwm_interface.cir

**Options to Evaluate:**
1. Increase capacitance to 47-100pF (fc = 66-33MHz) for better HF rejection
2. Add ferrite bead (50-300Ω @ 100MHz) in series with signal
   - Example: Murata BLM15AG102SN1 (1kΩ @ 100MHz, <1Ω DC)
3. Combination: 51Ω + 47pF + ferrite bead

**Verification Required:**
- SPICE simulation with improved filter designs
- Compare noise rejection @ 100MHz, 200MHz, 500MHz
- Verify rise/fall time impact remains <50ns
- Verify propagation delay increase remains acceptable
- Check GPIO loading stays within limits

**Trade-offs:**
- Higher capacitance: Better HF filtering but slower edges
- Ferrite bead: Excellent HF filtering, minimal timing impact, adds cost/BOM

**Trigger for Implementation:**
- Hardware testing shows EMI issues or false triggering
- EMC compliance testing reveals excessive emissions
- Operation near switch node requires extra immunity

**Reference:**
- Original verification: simulation/results/sim_10_esp32_ucc21550_pwm_verification.md (Section 5: Noise Immunity)
- Parent task: temper-vkx.1 (completed)

title: Verify components and create modular ngspice models
description: Each simulation should be performed in ngspice first and turned into a black box module according to its datasheet and verified as much as possible. It should be as modular as possible but also use the kicad_sym and lib files that exist. It is possible they contain errors so verify their correctness first.

title: Verify ADUM1250 model and symbol
description: Verify ngspice model and KiCad symbol for ADUM1250 against datasheet. Ensure modularity.

title: Verify IKW40N120H3 model and symbol
description: Verify ngspice model and KiCad symbol for IKW40N120H3 against datasheet. Ensure modularity.

title: Verify LMR51420/LMR51430 model and symbol
description: Verify ngspice model and KiCad symbol. Note: Folder is LMR51420, Datasheet is LMR51430. Clarify and verify.

title: Verify UCC14140/UCC14140-Q1 model and symbol
description: Verify ngspice model and KiCad symbol for UCC14140 against datasheet. Ensure modularity.

title: Verify UCC21550 model and symbol
description: Verify ngspice model and KiCad symbol for UCC21550 against datasheet. Ensure modularity.

title: Verify MAX31865 model and symbol
description: Verify ngspice model and KiCad symbol for MAX31865 against datasheet. Ensure modularity.

title: Document bootstrap vs UCC14140-Q1 gate driver power decision
description: Resolve the clarification from COMPONENT_COMPATIBILITY_VERIFICATION.md regarding gate driver power architecture. Document trade-offs between bootstrap supply (simpler, 45-50% duty cycle limit) vs UCC14140-Q1 isolated supply (more complex, no duty cycle limit). Make architectural decision and update relevant lessons (07, 08) in curriculum.

title: Create LDO selection and verification task
description: Select specific LDO part number for 5V→3.3V conversion (currently TBD in temper-ew8.3). Requirements: 380mA input capability, low noise (<50µV RMS), good PSRR, thermal performance. Verify against ESP32-S3 power requirements and create ngspice model. Candidates: TI TPS7A91, LT3042, or similar.

title: Document safety interlock requirements and implementation
description: Define all safety interlocks required for induction cooker: pan presence detection, overheat shutdown, overcurrent protection, HV bus overvoltage/undervoltage, ESP32 watchdog, gate driver UVLO. Specify response times, redundancy requirements, and fail-safe states. Map to hardware (Lesson 22) and firmware (Lesson 31) implementations.

title: Level 2: Gate Driver Power & Control Integration
description: Integrate gate driver power supply (UCC14140-Q1 or bootstrap) with UCC21550 gate drivers and ESP32-S3 control. Verify proper gate drive voltage levels (15V nominal), dead-time generation, propagation delays, and UVLO protection. Resolve bootstrap vs isolated supply configuration question from compatibility report.

title: Resolve gate driver power architecture (UCC14140 vs bootstrap)
description: Review Lesson 08 curriculum content and datasheet recommendations to determine intended gate driver power configuration. Document trade-offs: UCC14140-Q1 isolated supply (no duty cycle limit, more complex) vs bootstrap (simpler, <95% duty cycle limit). Recommend configuration for 45-50% duty cycle induction cooker application. Update COMPONENT_COMPATIBILITY_VERIFICATION.md with final decision.

title: Verify UCC14140-Q1 isolated DC/DC for gate driver bias
description: Simulate UCC14140-Q1 isolated DC/DC converter (12V input → 15V output for gate drive). Verify output voltage regulation, load transient response with gate driver switching, isolation barrier integrity, and thermal performance. Include input/output filtering per datasheet Section 8.2. Test under realistic gate charge current pulses (12mA average, 6.8A peak).

title: Verify UCC21550 dead-time generation and propagation delays
description: Simulate UCC21550 dead-time generation circuit with programmable DT settings (700-1000ns range). Verify dead-time accuracy, propagation delay matching between channels A/B (33ns typical), delay variation with temperature/voltage, and jitter characteristics. Confirm dead-time > t_fall(IGBT) + t_delay + margin per calculation in compatibility report.

title: Verify complete gate driver subsystem integration
description: Integrate ESP32-S3 PWM signals → UCC21550 gate drivers → dummy gate load (240nC charge). Verify end-to-end timing: ESP32 PWM generation → RC filter → gate driver logic → output stage → gate charge delivery. Measure total propagation delay, verify UVLO protection, test fault disable response time (48ns spec). Include realistic power supply sequencing.

title: Level 1: Digital Control Interfaces
description: Verify ESP32-S3 interfaces to all peripherals: UCC21550 gate driver (PWM signals with RC filtering), MAX31865 RTD converter (SPI), and ADUM1250 I2C isolator. Verify signal integrity, timing margins, proper pull-ups/pull-downs, and noise immunity per datasheet recommendations.

title: Verify ESP32-S3 to UCC21550 PWM interface with RC filtering
description: Simulate ESP32-S3 GPIO (3.3V CMOS) driving UCC21550 INA/INB inputs through recommended 51Ω + 33pF RC filter. Verify logic level thresholds (VIH/VIL margins), signal rise/fall times, propagation delay impact, noise immunity, and EMI filtering. Test with 20-50kHz PWM at various duty cycles.

title: Verify ESP32-S3 SPI to MAX31865 RTD interface
description: Simulate ESP32-S3 SPI interface (3.3V, up to 5MHz) to MAX31865 RTD converter. Verify SPI mode compatibility (mode 1 or 3), clock frequency margins, setup/hold times, signal integrity on MOSI/MISO/SCLK/CS lines. Include recommended series resistors and trace length considerations from MAX31865 datasheet Section 10.2.

title: Verify ESP32-S3 I2C through ADUM1250 isolator
description: Simulate ESP32-S3 I2C interface (3.3V, 100-400kHz) through ADUM1250 digital isolator to isolated I2C slaves. Verify pull-up resistor values (4.7kΩ recommended), capacitive loading limits, propagation delay symmetry, and isolation barrier integrity. Test with realistic I2C transaction sequences.

title: Verify ESP32-S3 ADC interface for analog sensing
description: Simulate ESP32-S3 ADC inputs (12-bit, 0-3.3V) for current sense, temperature sense, and voltage monitoring signals. Verify input impedance loading effects, anti-aliasing filter design (RC time constants), ADC sampling rate vs signal bandwidth, and quantization noise impact on control loop stability.

title: Verify power supply decoupling for all digital interfaces
description: Simulate power supply decoupling network for ESP32-S3, UCC21550 VCC, MAX31865 VDD, and ADUM1250 VDD1/VDD2. Verify capacitor placement, ESR/ESL effects, decoupling effectiveness across frequency range (DC to 100MHz), and PDN impedance. Follow ESP32-S3 hardware design guidelines Section 2.2 and individual IC datasheet recommendations.
